question: Operators flagged a short window of unusual top-of-winding temperature readings on T-014 over the last few days. First identify the sensor channels installed on this asset, then run an anomaly check and trend analysis on the winding temperature channel (winding_temp_top_c) to determine whether the deviations form a single burst or a sustained shift.
answer:
The sensor channels installed on T-014 are winding_temp_top_c, in addition to load_current_a, oil_temp_c, power_factor, voltage_hv_kv, and voltage_lv_kv.

The anomaly check on the winding temperature sensor detected 6 anomalous readings within the last 72 hours, which is 0.83% of total readings. These readings were on January 6, 12, 13, 16, 17, and 28.

The trend analysis of the winding temperature sensor showed that the temperature was stable over the last 30 days, with a mean value of 79.62°C and a slope per day of 0.046413. The R-squared value was 0.0007, indicating a very weak linear relationship between the temperature and time.
